codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the product detail page covering all required functionality: full-width product image, title, price in $XXX.XX format, remaining quantity, description, back button, and two action buttons (For sale outline, Purchase gradient) with confirmation dialogs. API route properly handles 404 and 500 errors. Tests are comprehensive with 3 API tests and 9 component tests covering happy path, error states, dialog open/close, and back navigation. The original commit had incorrect Next.js 15 async params typing (used synchronous `{ params: { id: string } }` instead of `Promise<>`), which required a separate fix commit (f4b9e8b). The current on-disk code is correct. Minor non-blocking issues include lack of keyboard dismiss (Escape key) on dialogs and duplicated dialog markup that could be extracted into a reusable component.

issues.description: Dialog overlay lacks keyboard dismiss support. Neither dialog handles the Escape key to close. Users relying on keyboard navigation cannot dismiss the dialog without clicking the OK button. Consider adding onKeyDown handler for Escape.

Duplicated dialog markup — the Purchase and For sale confirmation dialogs are nearly identical (differing only in the message text and close handler). Could be extracted to a reusable ConfirmationDialog component. Not blocking but increases maintenance surface.

Dialog overlay click-to-close not implemented. Clicking the backdrop (bg-black bg-opacity-50 overlay) does not close the dialog. Common UX pattern is to allow overlay click dismissal in addition to the OK button.
Uses native instead of Next.js component. However, this is consistent with the rest of the codebase (no next/image imports found in any file), so this is acceptable as a codebase convention.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker originally wrote the API route with synchronous params typing `{ params }: { params: { id: string } }` instead of the Next.js 15 async Promise pattern documented in AGENTS.md. This required a separate follow-up fix commit (f4b9e8b). The AGENTS.md convention note ('In Next.js 15, dynamic route params are async Promises. Type as `Promise<{param: type}>` and `await` before use') exists but the worker missed it during implementation. The convention could be made more prominent or include a code example showing both API route handler and page component signatures.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Original commit af74da6 had `{ params }: { params: { id: string } }` in app/api/products/[id]/route.ts:6 and app/products/[id]/page.tsx:19. Fixed in f4b9e8b to use Promise<> pattern. AGENTS.md has the rule but it's a single line in the Coding Conventions section.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The codebase consistently uses native tags instead of Next.js component across all pages (goods home, product detail). This is an implicit convention that is not documented in AGENTS.md. Future workers may try to use next/image and introduce inconsistency.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No imports of 'next/image' found anywhere in app/ directory. Product detail page uses at app/products/[id]/page.tsx:116, goods home components also use .

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker reported followedProcedure: true with no deviations. The skill procedure was followed correctly: read feature, check preconditions, write tests first, implement API then UI, run checks, manual verification with agent-browser, commit. No skill gaps identified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback shows followedProcedure: true, empty deviations and suggestedChanges arrays. Transcript skeleton confirms TDD approach and agent-browser verification.

summary: Product detail page implementation passes review. All required functionality is present and working: product information display (image, title, price, quantity, description), back navigation, two action buttons with correct styling (For sale outline, Purchase gradient), and confirmation dialogs with proper dismiss behavior. API endpoint correctly handles valid IDs, 404s, and errors. Test coverage is comprehensive (12 tests). The main issue was incorrect Next.js 15 async params typing in the original commit, which required a follow-up fix — this suggests the AGENTS.md convention on async params could be made more prominent. Non-blocking issues include missing keyboard/overlay dialog dismiss and duplicated dialog markup.
